what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG outline home icon using a currentColor stroke and no fill.
observations: Uses the SVG namespace and a viewBox of 0 0 24 24.
Draws the icon with one path containing roof, wall, and doorway line segments.
Uses fill="none" and stroke="currentColor" so the icon inherits surrounding color.
Sets stroke width to 1.5 with round line caps and round line joins.
standouts: Single path encodes the whole home outline.
The icon is color-themeable through currentColor.
conventions: Follows outline icon conventions with no fill, stroked geometry, and rounded stroke joins.
Does not define explicit width or height, relying on viewBox-based sizing.
concerns: No title, role, or aria attributes are present if the SVG is used standalone.
All geometry is in one path, so subparts cannot be styled independently without editing the path.
notes: A compact themeable SVG asset for a home/navigation icon.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the embedding context?
Is the lack of explicit width and height intentional for this asset?
Should the roof, body, and doorway remain a single path?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename implies a home icon, and the SVG content depicts a home outline.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable home icon that can inherit color and scale through its SVG viewBox.
contribution: Contributes a compact, currentColor-based home icon asset with a 24 by 24 outline style and minimal geometry.
summary_for_embedding: This is a home.svg asset defining a 24x24 no-fill currentColor stroked house outline icon in a single SVG path.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a home or main navigation destination as a reusable themeable vector icon.
